svg.text: MARLIE 1 — LAFAYETTE AI FACTORY & COMMAND CENTER
POWER DISTRIBUTION SCHEMATIC | FUEL TO CHIP | 4-LAYER POWER HIERARCHY | 1,300 kW IT
Sheet P-001 | Design Intent | 2026-04-02 | NOT FOR CONSTRUCTION

g.text: Cat Genset Controls | Auto-Sync | Emergency Only
Cat Genset emergency path only | 480V AC bus

svg.text: 800V DC MAIN DISTRIBUTION BUS — NVIDIA DSX REFERENCE
(DIFFERENT from Trappeys — Grid is BACKUP here, not sell-back)
LFP BATTERY BESS (600 kWh) battery bridge
Made 30 miles away in New Iberia, LA
Bloom Energy SOFC fuel cells (1.03 MW each, 800V DC Direct)
Henry Hub 40 miles — cheapest in USA
800V DC Direct output to 800V DC bus (no inverter, no AC stage)
~60% load per unit (1.24 MW on 2.06 MW)
Cloudy day + gas down = grid bridges
KEY DIFFERENCE: MARLIE 1 vs TRAPPEYS/WILLOW GLEN
Trappeys/Willow Glen: Grid = SELL-BACK only (Layer 4 revenue)
MARLIE 1: Grid = BACKUP power (Layer 4 safety net) — smaller facility, grid supports ops
1. Gas is BACKBONE (Layer 2) — always running, carries full facility load. Solar offsets but does not replace gas.
2. Grid (LUS + SLEMCO) is BACKUP (Layer 4) — different from Trappeys where grid is sell-back only.
3. DUAL POWER STACK: Eaton (facility) + Delta (rack). Delta 660 kW Rack (6x 110 kW, 480 kW BBU). e-Fuse SiC <3 us. 90 kW DC/DC 800V->50V MGX.
4. N+1 redundancy: 2x Bloom Energy SOFC (1.03 MW each, 800V DC Direct) for 1.24 MW load. Either unit offline = zero impact.
5. Solar DC-Direct: First Solar Series 7 TR1, buck to 800V bus at 97% eff. Eaton BESS 600 kWh ride-through.
6. All equipment American-made. Complete power chain: generator -> Eaton rectifier -> Delta rack power -> NVIDIA GPU.
